steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Subtracting the second polynomial changes the sign of every term in it.
$$(8x^3-5x+3)-(4x^2+9x-6)$$
$$=8x^3-5x+3-4x^2-9x+6$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Combine the linear terms and the constants. $$8x^3-4x^2+(-5x-9x)+(3+6)$$ $$8x^3-4x^2-14x+9$$ The choices may list terms in a different order, so compare like terms instead of only reading left to right. This is the same expression as $-4x^2+8x^3-14x+9$. The answer is C.
